source_jobs.title: Senior Social Media Specialist
source_jobs.description:
At FTMO, we believe that company growth starts with people. We are a team that pushes forward together, supports one another, and celebrates shared achievements. Our environment creates space for talents to grow – individually, as a team, and across the whole company.

As our senior Social Media Specialist, you won’t just follow a plan, you’ll write the playbook. We’re giving you the creative freedom to produce content that stops the scroll and starts conversations. You’ll be the heartbeat of our online presence, engaging directly with our audience to build a brand they truly love. If you’re a proactive storyteller who knows how to turn "likes" into meaningful growth, we want to see what you can do.



WHAT WILL BE YOUR AGENDA?

 - Social Media Content Creation & Implementation: You will help define what content we create, why, and for whom. You will help guide the content creation process and collaborate on bringing engaging campaigns to life.

 - Platform-Specific Content: Collaborating on tailored content across our key platforms, such as Instagram, LinkedIn, YouTube, X, FB, Reddit, and Discord, ensuring the right message fits the right audience.

 - Performance Monitoring & Optimization: Executing A/B testing and data analysis. You will use these insights to suggest actionable improvements and help shape our formats, tone, and channel priorities.

 - Community Management Oversight: Providing high-level guidance and collaborating with colleagues who are responsible for the daily community management.

 - Trend Spotting: identifying viral moments and responding to current events in the trading and market space.

 - Team Collaboration & Tools: Daily communication with the creative and content teams; tools used include Asana, Meta Business Suite, Elastic Email, HubSpot, Figma, Gemini, and YouTube Studio.


WHAT DO WE EXPECT FROM YOU?

 - 4+ years of proven experience in social media management and strategy, ideally in fintech, finance, or an international tech environment.

 - Experience with platforms such as Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, Twitter (X), YouTube, Reddit and TikTok.

 - Ability to communicate fluently in both Czech and English, as we are an international team.

 - Excellent communication skills – both verbal and written.

 - Creative thinking combined with strong organizational abilities.

 - Ability to manage discussions effectively and respond to user feedback constructively.


WHY JOIN THE FTMO TEAM?

 - We are a Czech fintech that, since 2015, has grown from an idea into a global project. 🚀

 - 300+ amazing teammates. We’re a great team who learn from each other every day.🤜🤛

 - How do we work? We focus on meaningful work and open communication, while only adopting processes that make our lives easier.

 - Prague, Národní třída. Enjoy our modern offices at the Quadrio shopping center, offering beautiful views and excellent accessibility.

 - What if I don’t trade? No worries. We’ll show you what our product is all about and introduce you to the basics of trading.

 - Free fruit, snacks, and coffee are always within reach in the office.

 - How do we promote strong relationships and well-being? Company cottage, team building events, and running club.

 - Flexible hybrid model. We prefer collaborating in person to keep the team spirit high, but we offer the flexibility you need to stay balanced.
   

*The benefits mentioned above apply to on-site employees in our Prague office.

source_jobs.title: Brand Specialist (Senior)
source_jobs.description:
At FTMO, we believe that company growth starts with people. We are a team that pushes forward together, supports one another, and celebrates shared achievements. Our environment creates space for talents to grow – individually, as a team, and across the whole company.

We are looking for a Senior Brand Specialist who will take ownership of how our brand resonates within a specific market segment. Your job is to ensure that FTMO’s reputation for expertise and trust is effectively translated for these audiences. You will be the bridge between our global "umbrella" brand and the specific expectations of these traders, making sure our positioning is always credible, educational, and backed by real market data.

FTMO is a global leader in the trading industry, known for setting high standards in trader education and support. To maintain this position, we need to constantly refine how we communicate our values to different audiences.




WHAT CAN YOU EXPECT?

 - Become the go-to expert on the behavior, needs, and standards of specific groups of traders.

 - Use market research and consumer insights to support every branding move. No guessing, we rely on facts and real feedback.

 - Identify which parts of the FTMO brand must stay the same and which should be subtly adjusted to different types of traders and their needs.

 - You will ensure that our brand key messaging is not only correctly set up but also clearly understood and well-received by the community.

 - Act as a guardian of our global identity, making sure that any audience-specific communication still feels 100% like FTMO.

 - Work closely with Product, Product Marketing and Marketing to ensure that what we build aligns perfectly with what we say.

 - Monitor the competition and market trends to identify new opportunities and stay ahead.

 - Set up and evaluate market data measurements to track how the brand is performing in the segment.

 - Prioritize tasks and initiatives to maximize business impact while keeping our brand positioning sharp and professional.




WHAT DO WE EXPECT FROM YOU?

 - 4+ years of experience in Brand Management or Strategic Marketing.

 - Fluent English (is a must) and Czech/Slovak.

 - Proven track record of adapting a global brand to local audiences or specific market segments.

 - Proven experience working with data and consumer insights to build brand strategies.

 - Strong analytical skills with the ability to turn consumer data and insights into actionable brand strategies.

 - Strong understanding of branding principles and consumer behavior.

 - Exceptional strategic thinking, problem-solving skills, and organizational skills.

 - Creative mindset and a passion for staying up-to-date with industry trends.

 - Basic AI usage is expected. However, the ability to use complex prompting (e.g., in Claude) for deep market research, building custom workflows, or automating daily tasks is a massive advantage.

 - Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.


WHY JOIN THE FTMO TEAM?

 - We are a Czech fintech that, since 2015, has grown from an idea into a global project. 🚀

 - 300+ amazing teammates. We’re a great team who learn from each other every day.🤜🤛

 - How do we work? We focus on meaningful work and open communication, while only adopting processes that make our lives easier.

 - Prague, Národní třída. Enjoy our modern offices at the Quadrio shopping center, offering beautiful views and excellent accessibility.

 - What if I don’t trade? No worries. We’ll show you what our product is all about and introduce you to the basics of trading.

 - Free fruit, snacks, and coffee are always within reach in the office.

 - How do we promote strong relationships and well-being? Company cottage, team building events, and running club.

 - Flexible hybrid model. We prefer collaborating in person to keep the team spirit high, but we offer the flexibility you need to stay balanced.

source_jobs.title: CRM Manager
source_jobs.description:
At FTMO, we believe that company growth starts with people. We are a team that pushes forward together, supports one another, and celebrates shared achievements. Our environment creates space for talents to grow – individually, as a team, and across the whole company.

In partnership with OANDA, we are looking for a CRM Manager to be a part of a global Marketing Team and you will report to the Head of CRM in London. This isn't a role for a passenger. We are looking for an A-player: someone who doesn't just execute tasks but takes the lead, challenges the status quo, and builds world-class regional and global ecosystems. You will be the architect of the Client lifecycle, using data to build sophisticated, multi-channel journeys, and in-life campaigns that drive impact on a global scale.





WHAT WILL YOU DO?

 - Design, execute, and optimise lifecycle and in-life campaigns to drive conversions across the Client’s life stages.

 - Act as a central pillar of the organisation; you will work cross-collaboratively with Marketing, Sales, Client Experience, Data, and Product Teams to align CRM efforts with broader business goals.

 - Create clear, concise briefs that inspire our Creative and Content Teams.

 - Evaluate current workflows, confidently challenge existing processes, and suggest/implement new ways of working to drive efficiency.

 - Maintain a strong understanding of marketing compliance and execute every campaign with absolute excellence and attention to detail.

 - Analyse data, report on KPIs, and present your results and roadmap in team meetings and to wider stakeholders.




WHAT DO YOU BRING TO THE TABLE?

 - 7+ years of hands-on experience, but impact matters more than years.

 - Proven track record in multi-channel strategy (Email, Push, In-App, SMS).

 - Strong experience with CRM orchestration platforms - Optimove is highly preferred, but experience with Salesforce or Braze is also a plus.

 - Demonstrated ability to take the initiative, lead cross-functional projects and influence stakeholders.

 - Strategic thinking with a "perfectionist" approach to execution and compliance.

 - Passion for the Client experience and dedication to creating impactful, relevant journeys.

 - Knowledge of trading is an advantage: Background in trading/FinTech sector or highly regulated global industries is a plus, but we’re happy to show you the ropes!
   
   
   


WHY JOIN THE FTMO TEAM?

 - We are a Czech fintech that, since 2015, has grown from an idea into a global project. 🚀

 - 300+ amazing teammates. We’re a great team who learn from each other every day.🤜🤛

 - How do we work? We focus on meaningful work and open communication, while only adopting processes that make our lives easier.

 - Prague, Národní třída. Enjoy our modern offices at the Quadrio shopping center, offering beautiful views and excellent accessibility.

 - What if I don’t trade? No worries. We’ll show you what our product is all about and introduce you to the basics of trading.

 - Free fruit, snacks, and coffee are always within reach in the office.

 - How do we promote strong relationships and well-being? Company cottage, team building events, and running club.

 - Flexible hybrid model. We prefer collaborating in person to keep the team spirit high, but we offer the flexibility you need to stay balanced.
   

*The benefits mentioned above apply to on-site employees in our Prague office.

source_jobs.title: AI Education Specialist
source_jobs.description:
At FTMO, we believe that company growth starts with people. We are a team that pushes forward together, supports one another, and celebrates shared achievements. Our environment creates space for talents to grow – individually, as a team, and across the whole company.

And currently, we are looking for someone to own the internal learning programs that make FTMO smarter and safer. You will build company-wide AI fluency, keep us security-aware in a high-target industry, unlock real value from the SaaS tools we already pay for, and grow role-specific capability in partnership with HR.

This is a founder-style role. You start hands-on, prove what works, and lay the foundation for an Education function that grows into a dedicated unit under HR over time. This role is designed to grow into that unit's lead.




WHAT WILL BE YOUR AGENDA?


 - Own FTMO's AI adoption program: practical literacy training, hands-on workshops, a working library of prompts and use cases, and a cross-department AI champion network.

 - Own the company-wide security awareness program in partnership with the Security team on phishing simulations, role-based training for finance and payouts, and refreshers tuned to threats.

 - Drive SaaS tool enablement. Look at how the tools we pay for are actually used, run workshops on the under-used features that save people time, and partner with tool owners, IT, and vendors.

 - Partner with HR on the competency framework: map training to the capabilities each role needs, help managers spot real skill gaps, and feed insights from performance cycles back into the plan.

 - Build and maintain a living learning library: needs assessments, design that actually works for adults, clean version control, and a review cadence.

 - Introduce, implement, and operate a learning management system, and use it to bring together external courses, vendor-led training, internal experts, and certifications.

 - Run change management around new tools and policies with a real launch plan, communication, materials, and the post-launch reinforcement.

 - Measure impact in a way leadership can act on and report quarterly (we use the Kirkpatrick four-level model as the shared scorecard).
   


WHAT WILL YOU BRING TO THE TABLE?


 - You have built and run internal learning or enablement programs end to end: needs assessment, design, delivery, and honest measurement of whether it worked.

 - At least one adoption or change program for a new tool or technology where you can show the before and after.

 - You use AI tools daily and can teach others to use them well.

 - You know how to design learning for adults and get the materials finished and applied.

 - A solid grasp of security awareness essentials: phishing, social engineering, business email compromise, and the specific risks for finance and payouts teams.

 - You can read SaaS usage analytics and turn the data into a workshop plan, and run multiple programs in parallel.

 - You facilitate well in a small room and in front of the full company, write clearly, and influence across departments and seniorities.

 - You hunt for the next training need before someone asks, and you mentor and coach naturally.

 - Czech and English at C1 or higher.
   


WHAT WOULD BE NICE TO HAVE?


 - Experience in financial services, fintech, or another regulated industry.

 - Closeness to a security awareness program or direct work with a Security team.

 - A degree in Education, HR, Psychology, Business, or similar, or any formal L&D credential.
   


WHY JOIN THE FTMO TEAM?

 - We are a Czech fintech that, since 2015, has grown from an idea into a global project. 🚀

 - 300+ amazing teammates. We’re a great team who learn from each other every day.🤜🤛

 - How do we work? We focus on meaningful work and open communication, while only adopting processes that make our lives easier.

 - Prague, Národní třída. Enjoy our modern offices at the Quadrio shopping center, offering beautiful views and excellent accessibi

source_jobs.title: Senior Security Analyst
source_jobs.description:
At FTMO, we believe that company growth starts with people. We are a team that pushes forward together, supports one another, and celebrates shared achievements. Our environment creates space for talents to grow – individually, as a team, and across the whole company.

In the Senior Security Analyst role, you will be the frontline defender of FTMO’s digital assets and IT infrastructure, ensuring our systems stay secure and operational. Your main goal is to proactively protect us from cyber threats, handle security incidents as they arise, and ensure we comply with all necessary internal and regulatory policies.




WHAT WILL YOU DO?

 - Scan our infrastructure and applications to identify security vulnerabilities, and work with system owners to get them fixed.

 - Continuously design and improve security playbooks and BAU security operations processes.

 - Manage security incidents from early detection through post-mortem reviews, following established playbooks.

 - Create and share training materials to help our employees defend against phishing, malware, and other everyday security threats.

 - Evaluate security risks and build standards, procedures, and controls to effectively manage them.

 - Lead security meetings, present clear action plans to senior management, and execute on those plans.

 - Keep all technical documentation, security policies, and "as is/to be" posture records fully up to date.
   


WHAT DO YOU BRING TO THE TABLE?

 - A university degree (or equivalent education) in computer science, computer engineering, or a related field.

 - 5 to 10 years of specific experience working in the information security industry.

 - Hands-on experience working with security technologies and implementing internal information security processes.

 - A background in navigating compliance and regulations within the financial sector.

 - Proven experience writing security guidelines, policies, standards, and processes.

 - Industry credentials such as CISSP, CISA, or CISM.

 - Certifications like Certified Ethical Hacker, CompTIA Network+, CWAPT, or Certified Reverse Engineering Analyst.

 - A solid working knowledge of industry security standards, including SOC2, ISO27001, and NIST.

 - Practical skills in ethical hacking and penetration testing to expose weak points before malicious hackers do.

 - Knowledge of computer forensics to collect data, analyze breaches, and prevent cybercrime.

 - The ability to use reverse engineering to patch software bugs or analyze malware.

 - Strong analytical and troubleshooting skills to monitor IT systems, diagnose technical problems, and draw conclusions from data.

 - Strong persuasive communication skills to effectively gain the support and commitment of other teams and management.


WHY JOIN THE FTMO TEAM?

 - We are a Czech fintech that, since 2015, has grown from an idea into a global project. 🚀

 - 300+ amazing teammates. We’re a great team who learn from each other every day.🤜🤛

 - How do we work? We focus on meaningful work and open communication, while only adopting processes that make our lives easier.

 - Prague, Národní třída. Enjoy our modern offices at the Quadrio shopping center, offering beautiful views and excellent accessibility.

 - What if I don’t trade? No worries. We’ll show you what our product is all about and introduce you to the basics of trading.

 - Free fruit, snacks, and coffee are always within reach in the office.

 - How do we promote strong relationships and well-being? Company cottage, team building events, and running club.

 - Flexible hybrid model. We prefer collaborating in person to keep the team spirit high, but we offer the flexibility you need to stay balanced.
   

*The benefits mentioned above apply to on-site employees in our Prague office.

source_jobs.title: Junior Lawyer
source_jobs.description:
At FTMO, we believe that company growth starts with people. We are a team that pushes forward together, supports one another, and celebrates shared achievements. Our environment creates space for talents to grow – individually, as a team, and across the whole company.

FTMO Group is looking for a Junior Lawyer to join our global legal and compliance team. If you have a feel for compliance, including data privacy and AI regulation, and want to apply it in a fast-moving fintech environment, we'd love to hear from you.

We are a team of 6 lawyers led by the Group Chief Legal Officer, with backgrounds from international law firms and regulated entities, and we are genuinely happy to share what we know. If you like getting things done, enjoy variety in your work, and want to grow fast in a fintech environment, this might be the right fit for you.




WHAT CAN YOU EXPECT?

Compliance (including data privacy, security and AI) - this will be the core of your agenda. As to the day-to-day work, this will include especially:

 - Research & Preparation of Legal Assessment and Business Advisory: A big part of your work will be legal research on a wide range of topics, discussions with business and assessing feasibility of new product ideas.

 - Policies: You will draft and review internal policies, making sure they are practical, up to date and aligned with our internal processes.

 - Regulatory Processes: You will participate in preparation and processing of various regulatory filings, especially relating to change management within the FTMO Group (overlap and coordination with M&A and corporate).

 - Product and Marketing: You will review product terms, advise on client communication and approve marketing campaigns and other content from a legal perspective, ensuring compliance with our group requirements.

 - Vendor Management & Contracts: You will review and negotiate vendor contracts as part of our vendor management process, making sure commercial agreements are solid and aligned with our standards.

 - KYC & Onboarding: You will be assisting in vendor onboarding processes, including more extensive KYC and due diligence processes where needed.

From time to time you will also get involved in other areas the team covers — such as corporate and M&A, real estate or labour law. We like people who are curious and willing to pitch in beyond their core agenda.




WHAT DO WE EXPECT FROM YOU?

 - A completed Master’s degree in Law.

 - Prior experience (e.g., as a legal assistant/paralegal during your studies) in a law firm or an in-house legal team is required.

 - Fluent knowledge of English and Czech (both spoken and written) is essential, as we communicate daily in an international environment and manage global projects.

 - A positive relationship with technology and an open mind when it comes to trying new innovative tools and working methods in particular AI tools such as Harvey AI or Claude.

 - Past experience in data protection, cybersecurity or AI related legal matters is an advantage.

 - A positive, proactive attitude, flexibility, and the ability to thrive in a dynamic, fast-paced environment.


WHY JOIN THE FTMO TEAM?

 - We are a Czech fintech that, since 2015, has grown from an idea into a global project. 🚀

 - 300+ amazing teammates. We’re a great team who learn from each other every day.🤜🤛

 - How do we work? We focus on meaningful work and open communication, while only adopting processes that make our lives easier.

 - Prague, Národní třída. Enjoy our modern offices at the Quadrio shopping center, offering beautiful views and excellent accessibility.

 - What if I don’t trade? No worries. We’ll show you what our product is all about and introduce you to the basics of trading.

 - Free fruit, snacks, and coffee are always within reach in the office.

 - How do we promote strong relationships and well-being? Company cottage, team building events, and running club.

 - Flexible hybrid model. We prefer collaborating in person

source_jobs.title: Anti-Fraud & Data Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At FTMO, we believe that company growth starts with people. We are a team that pushes forward together, supports one another, and celebrates shared achievements. Our environment creates space for talents to grow – individually, as a team, and across the whole company.

FTMO is launching a new crypto-native product. By design, it operates without traditional identity verification. This creates a fundamentally different security landscape: identities are cheap to create, coordination is easy to hide, and adversarial actors will find creative ways to exploit the system for profit.

This role owns the entire anti-fraud and data integrity layer. You'll design the detection systems, economic safeguards, and data infrastructure that make attacks expensive and unreliable. This is equal parts data engineering, adversarial thinking, and economic design, you're not just catching bad actors after the fact, you're making the system structurally hard to exploit.

To be direct: this is more of a hard problem to solve than a coding job. The challenge isn't writing pipelines, it's figuring out what the right detection approach even is, modeling whether it works economically, and then building it. You'll spend as much time thinking, researching, and modeling as you will writing code.

You won't be alone. There's an engineering team building the core platform alongside you, and the anti-fraud team will grow as the product scales. But you're laying the foundations, the architecture, the tooling decisions, the detection philosophy, that everything else builds on. What you design in the first months will shape how the platform defends itself for years.





WHAT WILL BE YOUR AGENDA?

 - Design and build a multi-layered fraud detection framework, combining on-chain data analysis, behavioral signals, statistical correlation, and economic mechanism design.

 - Build real-time detection pipelines that flag suspicious patterns as they happen.

 - Develop graph-based analysis of on-chain transaction flows, funding sources, destination clustering, shared history patterns, to identify coordinated activity across accounts.

 - Implement statistical correlation engines across active accounts to detect coordinated or adversarial strategies operating over time.

 - Design economic defense mechanisms that make attacks unprofitable by construction, this means modeling attack scenarios, running simulations, and tuning platform parameters before writing detection code.

 - Build a composite risk scoring system that aggregates signals from multiple detection layers into actionable decisions (monitoring, delayed processing, manual review, account restrictions).

 - Collect and analyze behavioral signals from the platform, frontend, session patterns, interaction fingerprints, timing analysis, as additional inputs to the scoring model.

 - Build internal tooling and dashboards for investigation and manual review.

 - Evaluate and select the tech stack for the anti-fraud domain: data pipeline infrastructure, graph storage, real-time processing, statistical analysis tooling. This is a greenfield decision, you propose, we discuss as a team.


WHAT WILL YOU BRING TO THE TABLE?

 - 3+ years in data engineering, fraud detection, security engineering, or quantitative analysis

 - Strong programming skills, Python, Rust, Go, or similar. We care about your ability to build production data systems, not a specific language.

 - Experience with graph databases or graph analysis techniques for relationship mapping and cluster detection.

 - Experience building data pipelines with streaming or real-time processing (Kafka, Redis Streams, or similar).

 - You think like an attacker. You can look at a system, find the exploit, and then design the defense. If you've never broken something to understand it, this role will frustrate you.

 - Comfort with ambiguity. There's no playbook for this, you'll be researching attack vectors, prototyping detection approaches, and iterating based on

source_jobs.title: Senior Corporate IT Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At FTMO, we believe that company growth starts with people. We are a team that pushes forward together, supports one another, and celebrates shared achievements. Our environment creates space for talents to grow – individually, as a team, and across the whole company.

We're looking for a Staff Corporate IT Engineer to serve as a technical leader within our IT organization - someone who keeps critical infrastructure running, mentors the team, and helps shape our technology direction.




WHAT WILL YOU DO?

You'll own the reliability and security of our core systems and infrastructure while acting as a go-to technical authority across the business. Day to day, that means:

 - Ensuring high availability, performance, and security of critical systems

 - Leading design sessions, retrospectives, and driving automation initiatives

 - Evaluating emerging technologies and contributing to FTMO's technology roadmap

 - Mentoring other Corporate IT Engineers and running training sessions

 - Translating complex technical topics for non-technical audiences

 - Providing technical oversight on Corporate IT infrastructure projects
   


WHAT DO YOU BRING TO THE TABLE?



 - 6+ years in systems administration (4+ in a senior/lead capacity)

 - Deep knowledge of Windows & macOS, networking (TCP/IP, SSL), and virtualisation

 - Solid Google Workspace administration knowledge

 - Understanding of IAM/PAM (SailPoint) concepts and practices

 - Scripting & automation skills (Python, PowerShell, Bash, Terraform, Ansible)

 - Experience with the Principle of Least Privilege (PoLP)

 - Excellent communication skills - proficiency in English (B2 level or higher)

 - Bachelor's degree in CS/IT or equivalent experience (Master's a plus)
   
   


NICE TO HAVES?

 - Linux experience

 - familiarity with security frameworks (ISO 27001, NIST)and disaster recovery planning

 - passion for AI and its potential to transform IT operations

 - Microsoft Entra ID knowledge

 - VDI experience (Workspot)

 - industry certifications such as MCSE, RHCE, Google Professional Cloud Architect, or Professional Workspace Administrator

 - Hands-on experience with cloud platforms (GCP preferred) and hybrid environments.
   


WHY JOIN THE FTMO TEAM?

 - We are a Czech fintech that, since 2015, has grown from an idea into a global project. 🚀

 - 300+ amazing teammates. We’re a great team who learn from each other every day.🤜🤛

 - How do we work? We focus on meaningful work and open communication, while only adopting processes that make our lives easier.

 - Prague, Národní třída. Enjoy our modern offices at the Quadrio shopping center, offering beautiful views and excellent accessibility.

 - How do we promote strong relationships and well-being? Company cottage, team building events, and running club.

*The benefits mentioned above apply to on-site employees in our Prague office.

source_jobs.title: AI Knowledge Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At FTMO, we believe that company growth starts with people. We are a team that pushes forward together, supports one another, and celebrates shared achievements. Our environment creates space for talents to grow – individually, as a team, and across the whole company.

Right now, we are looking for someone to own the design, content quality, and AI readiness of our group's internal knowledge base from a blank page. Your mission? Make sure both humans and AI agents find the right answer fast, and that teams across the group know how to build and run AI workflows on top of what we know.

This is a founder-style role. You start solo, prove the value, and lay the foundation for an AI Knowledge function that grows into a dedicated team over time. If you like owning a domain end to end and turning tribal knowledge into something a machine and a busy colleague can both trust, this role is tailored to you.




WHAT WILL BE YOUR AGENDA?

 - Stand up the group knowledge base as the single source of truth across the FTMO Group. Decide what belongs in it, how it is structured, and who owns each domain.

 - Design and maintain the information architecture (taxonomy, metadata, tagging, content models) so both employees and AI agents (RAG pipelines, internal copilots, MCP-based workflows) retrieve high-quality answers.

 - Run the content curation pipeline end to end with departmental SMEs: intake, drafting, review, freshness audits, deprecation. Set the editorial standard and hold the line.

 - Define what "AI-ready content" means in practice (chunking, structure, source attribution, freshness signals) and turn it into rules contributors can actually follow.

 - Wire the KB into the group's AI tooling stack together with the AI Lead and IT: internal RAG, Claude, AI agents, MCP connectors, and agentic search.

 - Build a network of knowledge champions inside each department, and train teams to query the KB effectively with AI and contribute back high-quality content.

 - Scope and build the team. Assess where one person stops being enough, write the business case, define the roles, hire, and grow into leading them while still doing hands-on work.

 - Track KB health (coverage, freshness, retrieval quality, agent answer accuracy, adoption, time saved) and govern access, sensitivity, and compliance together with Legal, Security, and Compliance.




WHAT WILL YOU BRING TO THE TABLE?

 - You have built and run a knowledge base, intranet, documentation function, or large content surface end to end: structure, content, governance, measurement.

 - At least one program where AI tools (RAG, copilots, agents) sit on top of structured content.

 - You use AI tools every day and can teach others. Practical fluency, hands-on with at least two of: ChatGPT, Claude, Microsoft 365 Copilot, Notion AI, Glean, Guru.

 - A working understanding of how RAG, embeddings, agentic workflows, and Model Context Protocol (MCP) behave in practice.

 - You design taxonomy, ontology, metadata, and content models that hold up under both human and machine consumption, and you have opinions about chunking, source attribution, and what AI-ready content actually looks like.

 - 5 to 7+ years in knowledge management, information architecture, technical writing, internal communications, content design, support operations, or L&D.

 - A founder mindset. Comfortable with a blank page, you scope, prototype, and ship value before asking for budget or headcount.

 - English at C1 or higher. Czech welcome.




WHAT WOULD BE NICE TO HAVE?

 - Prior direct lead experience (1 to 3 reports).

 - Experience in fintech, financial services, or another regulated industry.

 - A background in library or information science, taxonomy design, or content operations at scale, plus facilitation or training experience across multiple departments or sites.


WHY JOIN THE FTMO TEAM?

 - We are a Czech fintech that, since 2015, has grown from an idea into a global project. 🚀

 - 300+ amazing teammates.

source_jobs.title: Copywriter (Senior)
source_jobs.description:
At FTMO, we believe that company growth starts with people. We are a team that pushes forward together, supports one another, and celebrates shared achievements. Our environment creates space for talents to grow – individually, as a team, and across the whole company.

The Senior Copywriter owns copy across our web platforms, landing pages, and the trader app, and coordinates translations to keep messaging accurate and consistent in every language. Your goal is to create high-quality, persuasive content that drives user engagement, while ensuring copy, translation, and localization processes run smoothly across the company.




WHAT CAN YOU EXPECT?

 - Campaign and Landing Page Copywriting: Writing and editing copy for marketing campaigns and landing pages built in Framer and Webflow, including copy for campaign banners.

 - Website Copywriting and Management: Creating and maintaining website copy, and managing content updates via WordPress.

 - Trader App Copywriting and Content Management: Writing and maintaining in-app copy and managing related content for the trading platform.

 - Cross-functional Collaboration: Working closely with the multimedia and UX teams to ensure copy supports design and user experience goals.

 - Translations Coordination: Coordinating the translation pipeline and workflows across languages.

 - Czech Translations, Proofreading and Localization: Translating content into Czech, proofreading, and adapting content for local audiences.

 - Cross-language Consistency: Maintaining a consistent tone, terminology, and style across all language versions.

 - Ad Hoc Projects: Providing copywriting support for ad hoc and cross-departmental projects as needed.
   
   


WHAT DO WE EXPECT FROM YOU?

 - Excellent command of Czech and English.

 - Experience with WordPress and web/landing page builders such as Framer or Webflow is a must.

 - Ability to write clearly and persuasively, with the target audience in mind.

 - Strong sense of text structure, tone of voice, and ability to maintain consistency across formats and languages.

 - Independence, creativity, and a sense of responsibility for what you put out into the world.

 - Proven experience coordinating with cross-functional teams (multimedia, UX) and managing external translators or agencies.

 - Interest in trading and finance.


WHY JOIN THE FTMO TEAM?

 - We are a Czech fintech that, since 2015, has grown from an idea into a global project. 🚀

 - 300+ amazing teammates. We’re a great team who learn from each other every day.🤜🤛

 - How do we work? We focus on meaningful work and open communication, while only adopting processes that make our lives easier.

 - Prague, Národní třída. Enjoy our modern offices at the Quadrio shopping center, offering beautiful views and excellent accessibility.

 - What if I don’t trade? No worries. We’ll show you what our product is all about and introduce you to the basics of trading.

 - Free fruit, snacks, and coffee are always within reach in the office.

 - How do we promote strong relationships and well-being? Company cottage, team building events, and running club.

 - Flexible hybrid model. We prefer collaborating in person to keep the team spirit high, but we offer the flexibility you need to stay balanced.
   

*The benefits mentioned above apply to on-site employees in our Prague office.

source_jobs.title: Paralegal
source_jobs.description:
At FTMO, we believe that company growth starts with people. We are a team that pushes forward together, supports one another, and celebrates shared achievements. Our environment creates space for talents to grow – individually, as a team, and across the whole company.

FTMO Group is looking for a Paralegal to join our global legal and compliance team. We are a team of 6 lawyers led by the Group Chief Legal Officer, with backgrounds from international law firms and regulated entities, and we are genuinely happy to share what we know.

The main goal of this position is to handle the essential day-to-day administration and support workflows that keep our legal department running smoothly. You will be a key operational support across a broad range of tasks — from document management and corporate filings to drafting basic legal documents and managing vendor onboarding requests.

We are open to both full-time and part-time candidates; for part-time, we require daily availability of 4–8 hours.




WHAT CAN YOU EXPECT?

 - Providing general administrative support to the legal team including tracking deadlines, scanning, coordinating execution of documents, etc.

 - Managing the legal department's document database.

 - Tracking corporate changes across the FTMO Group (directorship changes, registered address updates, share transfers, etc.).

 - Supporting corporate and regulatory filings.

 - Drafting and managing routine legal documents such as Powers of Attorney, NDAs, and basic correspondence.

 - Managing end-to-end vendor onboarding process, including preparation of relevant forms, collecting and verifying required documentation and liaising with internal stakeholders (Finance, IT, …), potentially also assistance with contracts review (depending on level of expertise and experience).

 - Handling incoming KYC requests from vendors and other third parties and ensuring timely and accurate responses.

 - Assisting with legal research on straightforward matters as directed by the team.

 - We encourage using AI, automation, and modern workflows to work smarter and streamline your daily tasks.


WHAT DO WE EXPECT FROM YOU?

 - Minimum of a high school diploma.

 - No prior legal experience required; we will gladly teach you everything, though administrative or legal support background is a plus. We would especially welcome somebody with experience working in a law firm.

 - Good English and Czech (written & spoken) is essential, as you will handle Czech & international onboarding documents and digital systems daily.

 - You have a sharp eye for detail and the patience needed for routine compliance checklists and data entry.

 - You bring a proactive, helpful attitude and feel comfortable working in a fast-paced startup environment.


WHY JOIN THE FTMO TEAM?

 - We are a Czech fintech that, since 2015, has grown from an idea into a global project. 🚀

 - 300+ amazing teammates. We’re a great team who learn from each other every day.🤜🤛

 - How do we work? We focus on meaningful work and open communication, while only adopting processes that make our lives easier.

 - Prague, Národní třída. Enjoy our modern offices at the Quadrio shopping center, offering beautiful views and excellent accessibility.

 - What if I don’t trade? No worries. We’ll show you what our product is all about and introduce you to the basics of trading.

 - Free fruit, snacks, and coffee are always within reach in the office.

 - How do we promote strong relationships and well-being? Company cottage, team building events, and running club.

 - Flexible hybrid model. We prefer collaborating in person to keep the team spirit high, but we offer the flexibility you need to stay balanced.
   

*The benefits mentioned above apply to on-site employees in our Prague office.

source_jobs.title: Senior Digital Designer
source_jobs.description:
At FTMO, we believe that company growth starts with people. We are a team that pushes forward together, supports one another, and celebrates shared achievements. Our environment creates space for talents to grow – individually, as a team, and across the whole company.

FTMO, in partnership with OANDA (a global fintech company operating across 7 regulated entities and multiple languages) is looking for a Senior Digital Designer who can bridge the worlds of creative design and UX/UI design. You’ll be embedded in OANDA's award-winning in-house Creative Team — working on campaigns and digital experiences that reach traders around the world. We're looking for someone who thinks in systems, sweats the details, and knows how to turn a brief into a beautiful, high-performing digital experience.




WHAT WILL YOU DO?

 - Own front-end website design — creating experiences seen by traders globally across multiple languages and markets.

 - Work across global campaigns spanning 7 regulated entities and emerging markets,

 - from concept through to delivery.

 - Play a key role in developing and growing the OANDA brand — helping shape how it looks, feels, and performs across every digital touchpoint.

 - Use AI and modern design tools to accelerate ideation, improve efficiency, and push the quality of creative output forward.

 - Collaborate closely with Content Editors, Web Development, CRO, CRM, and Marketing to deliver seamless, user-centred experiences.

 - Continuously optimise user flows and creative performance user data, testing, and real insight.

 - Contribute to and evolve design systems that keep the brand consistent and scalable across all platforms.

 - Mentor junior designers and act as a senior guardian of brand quality and consistency.

 - Take full ownership of projects — from discovery and concept through to delivery and optimisation.

 - No trading background? No problem — we'll get you up to speed!
   


WHAT DO YOU BRING TO THE TABLE?

 - 5+ years of experience in UX/UI and Creative Design within an in-house or agency environment.

 - Expert proficiency in Figma and Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign, After Effects).

 - Experience using AI-powered tools (e.g. Claude) to support ideation, prototyping, and design workflows.

 - A strong portfolio demonstrating both user-centred design thinking and high-quality visual and creative execution.

 - Solid understanding of UX principles, accessibility, responsive design, and conversion-focused experiences.

 - Confidence working cross-functionally with developers, stakeholders, and content teams.

 - Strong communication skills — able to clearly articulate design decisions and influence outcomes.

 - High level of autonomy, ownership, and attention to detail.

 - Fluent English is a must (written and spoken).
   


WHAT WOULD BE NICE TO HAVE?

 - Experience in FinTech, Banking, FX, CFDs, or a similarly regulated industry.

 - Familiarity with analytics and experimentation tools (e.g. Hotjar, Optimizely).

 - Understanding of design systems, component-based design, and CMS platforms.

 - Basic knowledge of front-end principles (HTML/CSS).

source_jobs.title: AI Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At FTMO, we believe that company growth starts with people. We are a team that pushes forward together, supports one another, and celebrates shared achievements. Our environment creates space for talents to grow – individually, as a team, and across the whole company.

We are building the way FTMO ships software in the age of AI, and we want you to own it. This is not a "use a bit of Copilot" role. We are making AI-first delivery the default across our IT department, and you will be the engineer who makes that real.

You will own our AI development harness: the coding-agent patterns, conventions, and end-to-end loops that let agents make reliable, incremental progress across long sessions and many context windows. Think of it as building the AI software factory the whole company builds on top of.

This is a hands-on senior role for someone who already ships production-grade software primarily by directing coding agents, not by hand-writing every line. You set the example, the guardrails, and the conventions that every other engineer's agents will follow.




WHAT WILL BE YOUR AGENDA?


 - Design and own FTMO's AI development harness: initializer and coding-agent patterns, AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md conventions, progress and checkpoint files, feature lists, and end-to-end test loops.

 - Shape the AI-native SDLC and PDLC together with our DevEx Specialist. Map every phase to a clear delegate / review / own model, then wire the tooling (MCP integrations, eval loops, CI hooks).

 - Run fast proofs of concept against real codebases and workflows, then turn the ones that hold up into production-ready patterns.

 - Deliver production software agent-first, keeping the codebase clean and mergeable and setting the standard for agent-first delivery.

 - Build and harden the AI infrastructure: model access and routing, sandboxes, secrets and permissioning, observability, and cost controls for agent runs.

 - Raise the whole department's AI fluency through patterns, internal documentation, and hands-on pairing, and help non-engineering teams ship and govern their own apps.

 - Track the frontier. Evaluate new models and tooling, and bring the ones that earn their place into our stack, including reporting.
   


WHAT DO YOU BRING TO THE TABLE?


 - You are a senior SW engineer who has shipped and owned production software across the full lifecycle.

 - Around a year working AI-first: delivering real, production-grade software primarily by directing coding agents.

 - You can show us the harness. Agent configs, AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md files, eval suites, prompts, MCP integrations, or repositories that prove how you get agents to do reliable work.

 - Strong general software engineering. You can read, design, and review production code in at least one major stack and reason about architecture, testing, and security.

 - Deep, practical fluency with agentic coding tools (Claude Code and the Claude Agent SDK, Codex, Cursor, or equivalents): context management, compaction, long-running sessions, and multi-agent patterns.

 - Prompt and context engineering as a daily discipline, plus a feel for evals and verification loops so agent output is measured against tests, latency, and style

 - Ownership and influence. You take a problem from PoC to production, leave the environment clean, and get other engineers to adopt new ways of working without needing a mandate.

 - English at B2 level or above.
   


WHAT WOULD BE NICE TO HAVE?


 - Experience in fintech or another regulated, high-stakes environment.

 - Public contributions: open-source agent tooling, write-ups, or talks on AI-native development.

 - Hands-on AI infrastructure work: model routing, sandboxes, permissioning, observability, and cost control for agent runs at scale.


WHY JOIN THE FTMO TEAM?

 - We are a Czech fintech that, since 2015, has grown from an idea into a global project. 🚀

 - 300+ amazing teammates. We’re a great team who learn from each other every day.🤜🤛

 - How do we wor

Your goal is to keep the content pipeline running smoothly - from web administration and translation coordination to final proofreading - so that accurate, well-presented content reaches our traders on time.

source_jobs.title: Programmatic Media Buyer
source_jobs.description:
At FTMO, we believe that company growth starts with people. We are a team that pushes forward together, supports one another, and celebrates shared achievements. Our environment creates space for talents to grow – individually, as a team, and across the whole company.

We are looking for an experienced digital advertising expert to strengthen our marketing team and lead the full launch of our programmatic buying via Display & Video 360.

The ideal candidate has strong performance marketing expertise and hands-on experience with the Google Marketing Platform. You will have full support: top-tier creatives from our in-house design and video teams, advanced analytics from our BI department, and direct access to Google thanks to our key partner status.




WHAT AWAITS YOU IN THIS ROLE?

 - Launch and strategic management of programmatic advertising: You will be the key person to launch and set up processes for media buying via DV360.

 - Leading and optimizing performance campaigns with the goal of maximizing results.

 - Designing and implementing advanced automation strategies within RTB tools.

 - Developing the FTMO brand in new markets.

 - In-depth data analysis and identifying key insights for further optimizations.

 - Active collaboration with the team and bringing new ideas to improve campaign performance.

 - Opportunity to participate in strategic decisions and the creation of long-term plans.

 - Advanced data analysis (Campaign Manager 360, GA4) and uncovering key correlations for next-level optimization.




WHAT SKILLS SHOULD YOU HAVE?

 - Expertise in Google Marketing Platform (DV360, CM360): You understand account setup and architecture, Floodlights, bidding strategies, and frequency capping.

 - Buying Strategy: Experience with Open Auction, setting up PMP deals, and Programmatic Guaranteed.

 - Technical Knowledge: You understand ad specs (HTML5, VAST/VPAID) and can effectively brief our creative team.

 - Data & Brand Safety: Advanced audience management (1st/3rd party) and ensuring a safe brand environment (verification tools).

 - Advanced Analytics: understanding attribution models, post-view conversions, and Brand Lift studies.


WHY JOIN THE FTMO TEAM?

 - 300+ amazing teammates. We’re a great team who learn from each other every day.🤜🤛

source_jobs.title: CRM & Lead Management Specialist
source_jobs.description:
At FTMO, we believe that company growth starts with people. We are a team that pushes forward together, supports one another, and celebrates shared achievements. Our environment creates space for talents to grow – individually, as a team, and across the whole company.

Do you want to be the driving force behind how FTMO communicates with clients worldwide? Join our Content team! Your primary mission will be to fully unleash the potential of our CRM and marketing automation platforms by setting up email campaigns, designing automated workflows and segmenting client groups. In this role, you will also take full ownership of our lead management and lifecycle marketing initiatives.




WHAT CAN YOU EXPECT?

 - HubSpot is your playground: You have solid, hands-on experience with HubSpot (or a similar advanced CRM and marketing automation system). Setting up workflows, managing custom properties, and keeping our database architecture clean is second nature to you.

 - Architect of funnels & campaigns: You think in customer journeys. You can independently design automated email flows, segment smart lists, and continuously test and optimize how we communicate with our global community of traders.

 - Bilingual communication: We are a global fintech leader based in Prague. A strong command of both Czech (for smooth internal collaboration) and English (for our international audience) is essential to keep our localized messaging sharp and on-point.

 - Drive and data ownership: We move fast and value a proactive, independent work approach. You are someone who doesn't just launch a campaign and walk away; you take full ownership, monitor the data, troubleshoot errors, and see projects through to a successful finish.

 - An appetite for the trading world: Having a background in financial markets or prop trading is a massive plus. If you don't have it yet, don't worry, as long as you are curious and eager to learn, we are more than happy to show you the ropes!




WHAT DO WE EXPECT FROM YOU?

 - Experience with HubSpot is a huge plus: Previous hands-on experience with the HubSpot platform (or a similar advanced CRM and marketing automation system).

 - Understanding of online marketing: A solid understanding of digital marketing tools and a clear grasp of how modern customer communication works.

 - Email marketing & funnels: Ability to independently build email campaigns, work with funnel scenarios, and continuously optimize client communication.

 - A feel for languages: Strong command of both Czech and English.

 - Work approach: Reliability, a proactive mindset, drive, and the ability to see things through to successful completion.

 - Knowledge of trading is an advantage: Background in trading or financial markets is a plus, but we’re happy to show you the ropes!


WHY JOIN THE FTMO TEAM?

 - We are a Czech fintech that, since 2015, has grown from an idea into a global project. 🚀

 - 300+ amazing teammates. We’re a great team who learn from each other every day.🤜🤛

 - How do we work? We focus on meaningful work and open communication, while only adopting processes that make our lives easier.

 - Prague, Národní třída. Enjoy our modern offices at the Quadrio shopping center, offering beautiful views and excellent accessibility.

 - What if I don’t trade? No worries. We’ll show you what our product is all about and introduce you to the basics of trading.

 - Free fruit, snacks, and coffee are always within reach in the office.
